page: Amazon Web Services m7gd.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 processor architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz, this instance features 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory, translating to 4.0 GB of memory per core. The hardware configuration includes a bundled 3800 GB local NVMe SSD instance store and a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m7gd.metal demonstrates strong multi-core and memory performance. It achieves top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core scoring and software compilation using Clang, alongside a top-tier Passmark memory score. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 254.06 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 121.58 GB/sec for large block write operations. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top tier and strong categories, respectively, with Redis handling 103.12 million operations per second. However, single-threaded performance and text generation for small language models represent relative performance bottlenecks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal delivery model combined with energy-efficient ARM architecture provides high resource density and high memory bandwidth without hyperthreading overhead. The inclusion of 3800 GB of local NVMe storage eliminates the need for external storage attachments for high-throughput local data. This server is highly suited for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, high-performance database operations, static web serving, and memory-intensive caching layers.
description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 processor architecture running at 2.6 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory, a bundled 3800 GB local NVMe SSD, and 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance delivers top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks, software compilation, and database operations, supported by strong memory bandwidth of up to 254.06 GB/sec for large block reads. While single-threaded tasks and text generation for language models show average to poor performance, the server's high resource density and dedicated core allocation offer excellent qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded applications. It is highly suited for high-throughput database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and memory-intensive caching workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.metal is a bare-metal server with 64 Graviton physical cores, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 64 Graviton cores, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A bare-metal ARMv8 server combining sixty-four dedicated cores with high-speed DDR5 memory and local NVMe SSD storage for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 physical cores on ARMv8 architecture
256 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core performance, software compilation, and database operations
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Optimized for multi-threaded workloads, caching layers, and database hosting
